fields.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #1 (HIGH). Two clones can both claim the same open ticket (the write lock is per-clone). On merge the STATUS fork resolves deterministically (lower event UUID wins, `reducer/_processors.py` ~L145) while assignee resolves last-writer-wins by HLC — a *different* key — but **both agents were told CLAIMED** and proceed to duplicate work. The resolved fork is only a replay-time `logger.warning("PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED ...")` (`reducer/_processors.py` ~L159-167), surfaced by no user-facing command. There is also no test covering two-clone concurrent claim (audit 7.1): the claim-storm test in `test_store_concurrency.py::test_claim_storm_one_winner` is single-clone.

## What / files
1. **Post-claim verification** — `src/rebar/_commands/claim.py` `claim_compute` (~L170-175, after `push.push_after_commit`): re-read the ticket from the merged store and, if the winning STATUS event is not this clone's (assignee ≠ this claimant, i.e. our claim lost the fork), return the ConcurrencyMismatch signal (**exit 10**) with a clear "claim lost on cross-clone merge" message, so the loser learns its claim didn't stick once the merge is visible locally.
2. **Durable surfacing** — when the reducer resolves a STATUS fork (`reducer/_processors.py` ~L159-167), record a durable marker (reuse `alert_store.append`, `bridge_alerts` dir) in addition to the log line.
3. **fsck/validate/show** — `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py`: add a finding (following the `lines.append("<TAG>: <ticket>/... — <detail>")` + `issue_count` pattern, e.g. ~L174-181/L216-235) e.g. `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED: <ticket> — concurrent claim/status race resolved; dropped uuid=<...>`, read from the marker; surface the same on `show` as an annotation.
4. **Regression test** — `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` (extend `test_two_clone_union_deterministic_replay_and_fork_tiebreak` ~L188 or add a sibling using the `two_clones` fixture): two clones claim the same open ticket offline, push/merge both ways, assert (a) converged assignee is deterministic, (b) the loser can detect the loss (exit-10 path or the surfaced marker), (c) the fork is surfaced via alert/fsck.

## Scope
In: claim path (`_commands/claim.py`, `_commands/txn.py`), reducer fork marker (`reducer/_processors.py`), fsck/validate/show surfacing (`_commands/fsck.py`), tests. Out: changing the fork-resolution rule (lower-UUID-wins stays — docs I8); distributed locking.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A claim that loses the cross-clone race is reported to the loser (a retroactive exit-10 path, or an equivalent explicit signal) once the merge is visible locally — covered by a two-clone test
- [ ] A resolved STATUS fork appears as an fsck (or validate) finding and/or a `show` annotation on the affected ticket (durable marker, not just a log line)
- [ ] New two-clone concurrent-claim regression test in the concurrency suite: deterministic converged assignee + the loser detects the loss + the fork is surfaced
- [ ] docs/concurrency.md notes the surfacing behaviour
- [ ] `make test` green
